items.title: Giant Oil Supertanker Orders Eclipse Record Set in 2008
items.summary: The world’s shipowners have placed orders for a record number of new oil supertankers, surpassing a boom back in 2008 that ultimately led to a glut and a collapse in rates.

items.title: Lebanon’s intractable crisis pulls Israel and Iran back towards war
items.summary: Impasse over Hizbollah has repeatedly tested the fragile ceasefire between Tehran and Washington

items.title: Air France Could Consider Being Part of EasyJet Bid, CEO Says
items.summary: In an interview at the IATA annual meeting in Rio de Janeiro, Air France-KLM CEO Benjamin Smith said the airline may need to raise ticket prices to compensate for rising fuel costs sparked by war in the Middle East. The carrier is also looking to reopen routes to the region when  it's safer. Smith signaled he’d be willing to consider becoming involved in a bid for EasyJet Plc.  (Source: Bloomberg)

India’s Current Account Gap Beats Forecasts as Remittances Surge
items.summary: India’s current account fared better than expected in the year ended March, with the deficit staying flat as strong services receipts and higher remittances offset disruptions caused by the Iran war.

items.title: Texas Gas Drillers Shut Out of Oil Price Rally Turn to Shutting Off Wells
items.summary: Two extremes are currently playing out in the Permian Basin. Drillers are reaping the benefits of a historic price rally due to the Iran war. But when it comes to natural gas, they’re having to pay customers to take it away.

items.title: Ratcliffe’s Ineos Kicks Off Loan Deal After Iran War Boost
items.summary: Ineos Group started marketing new term loans to raise around $960 million, marking the second time in recent weeks that Jim Ratcliffe’s struggling chemicals conglomerate has tapped debt markets.

items.title: Europe’s Imports of Diesel and Jet Fuel Shrivel as War Drags On
items.summary: Europe’s imports of diesel and jet fuel stagnated for a second month in May, piling pressure on regional energy supplies as the Iran war grinds on.

items.title: Southwest COO on Strong Business Travel, Price Increases
items.summary: Southwest Airlines Chief Operating Officer Andrew Watterson says business travel has remained “incredibly strong” despite higher costs related to the War in Iran. He speaks with Lisa Abramowicz on the sidelines of the IATA Conference in Brazil. (Source: Bloomberg)

Schwab's Martin Says Fed Is Edging Toward a Rate Hike
items.summary: There's a case for the Federal Reserve to "hike right now,'' and even if policymakers aren't ready yet, the bar to action is definitely getting lower, according to Collin Martin at the Schwab Center for Financial Research. He speaks with Jonathan Ferro and Annmarie Hordern on Bloomberg Television's "Surveillance.'' (Source: Bloomberg)

items.title: Fed Seen With Case to ‘Hike Right Now’ as Bar to Act Gets Lower
items.summary: The bar for a Federal Reserve rate hike is falling as the job market remains robust in the face of stubborn price pressures, according to Collin Martin at the Schwab Center for Financial Research.

items.title: Iranians exhausted by prospect of more fighting with Israel
items.summary:
Residents of Tehran awoke on Monday anxious and drained by the prospect of full-scale war resuming, following tit-for-tat strikes between arch-foes Iran and Israel that marked the greatest threat to the fragile ceasefire thus far.
“We don’t know if there will be a war, nor if a peace deal will last,” said Maryam, a 41-year-old accountant in Tehran’s central Valiasr Square.

items.title: Resuming War Is a Short-Term Political Boon for Netanyahu, but Grim Choices Await
items.summary: If President Trump forces Israel to stand down against Iran, analysts say, it could leave Israel hindered from responding forcefully to attacks by Hezbollah.

items.title: Houthis Threaten to Widen Iran War by Blocking Israeli Shipping in Red Sea
items.summary: The Yemeni militia group, which is backed by Iran, said it would impose a partial blockade in the waterway, a move that would further strangle supply routes and aggravate the conflict in the Mideast.

items.title: The Iran War is Forcing Energy-Importing Countries to Turn Inward
items.summary: The Iran war is pushing countries to prioritize domestic energy in order to protect themselves from volatile oil and natural gas markets.

items.title: Iran-Israel Escalation Ignited in Lebanon, Which Is Trapped in Their Conflict
